results.name: has_hook
results.detail: "You can train and still be too *sedentary*."

rationales.hook_strength: "Train and still be too sedentary" is a genuine gotcha for gym-goers, and the enzyme tease earns the next swipe — docked one for no number in the headline.
rationales.clarity: Every slide is one idea with a specific, actionable takeaway; the 2–3 minute / 30–60 minute cadence is concrete enough to act on today.
rationales.audience_fit: Afternoon energy crash, desk job, already working out — this is a perfect mirror of the 20–35 tired-by-3pm persona.
rationales.novelty: The lipoprotein lipase angle is legitimately underexplained in mainstream content, though "move every 30 minutes" has been circulating for a few years.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are hedged ("can blunt"), no hypey verbs, CTA is functional and tied directly to the score product without cringe.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential — actionable, specific mechanism — but the visual hook depends heavily on cover design to stop the scroll against saturated "sitting is the new smoking" content.
